name: CONTRACT-BACKLOG-42
title: build-prompt.py orphan-check vs single-pass substitution leaks slot-value placeholders
description:
build-prompt.py's orphan-placeholder check (regex \{\{\s*[a-zA-Z_][a-zA-Z0-9_]*\s*\}\}) matches any double-brace name pattern in the assembled body, not just in the template body. Because substitution is single-pass, any slot value that contains literal double-brace text (e.g. spec.md prose describing the placeholder syntax, impl-suggestion JSON content referencing slot names) leaks through and trips the orphan check.

Made Phase C and Phase D dispatch fragile during CONTRACT-BACKLOG-1 implementation — multiple times had to defang spec/impl-suggestion text by replacing the literal placeholder syntax with backticked names to unblock dispatch.

Fix candidates:
(a) only check for orphans whose names are NOT in the declared slot set — substituted names by definition removed them; remaining unknown names are real orphans; this preserves the template-author safety net without false positives from slot values
(b) two-pass substitution where slot values are quoted/escaped before substitution and unescaped after
(c) require a distinct placeholder syntax in templates (e.g. {{$name}}) that is unlikely to occur in slot content

Option (a) is least disruptive and recommended.
